description: Recompute the Tononi-Edelman-inspired neural-complexity CN (integration x differentiation, identical proxy to e-0010) but over the CONCEPTUAL bridge matrix W_struct[i,j]=surprise(closest cross-cluster proof pair) from edit_distance_bridges, instead of the OPERATIONAL shared-premise matrix. Restricted to the 30 clusters the structural census scored. Compare the integrative hub and rank-correlate the two CN orderings (Spearman) over the 30 shared clusters.
motivation: a-0010 named SetTheory the operational (premise) integrative hub; a-0032 showed operational and conceptual bridges are near-independent signals. The natural follow-up: under the conceptual (proof-structure) lens, is the integrative hub still SetTheory? A cheap CPU-only recomputation reusing existing edit_distance_bridges + label data.
formal_results: Conceptual global CN=1.472 (operational 1.167). SetTheory (operational hub, CN_op=1.037) falls to rank 13/30 under the conceptual lens (CN_conc=1.106). Top conceptual CN hubs: Data cid53 (CN 3.14, diff 1.0), Data cid26 (2.55), RingTheory cid28 (2.44). Spearman CN conceptual-vs-operational rho=0.082 (p=0.667); Spearman integration rho=0.146 (p=0.442) over the 30 shared clusters. Broadest structural reach (integration max, connect to 29/30): MeasureTheory/Analysis/Topology cid1, LinearAlgebra cid5, Analysis cid9, Data cid6, Batteries cid23.
results_description: The two bridge classes disagree on which domain is most integrative: the conceptual and operational CN rankings are statistically uncorrelated (rho=0.08). SetTheory is the premise hub but only mid-pack structurally. The clusters with broadest structural reach are LinearAlgebra and Analysis, directly confirming a-0009's lingua-franca observation. Differentiation (module-based) is shared across both lenses, so the uncorrelated CN is driven by uncorrelated integration (structural reach != premise reach).
conclusions: The integrative hub is lens-dependent: SetTheory is the operational/premise hub (a-0010) but drops to rank 13/30 under proof-structure; conceptual integration is led by LinearAlgebra/Analysis. This is a hub-level corollary of a-0032's operational-vs-conceptual independence. Caveats: conceptual census limited to top-30 clusters (high-similarity sample); the top CN hub (small pure Data cluster, diff=1.0 on a tiny module set) is artifact-prone, so the robust claims are the rank uncorrelation and SetTheory's drop, not the precise #1.
